methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $11M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in AI & Software (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 13 same-sector peers exist in AI & Software, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Cortical Labs is a Series A-stage AI & Software company founded by Hon Weng Chong and Andy Kitchen that has raised $11M to date. The relevant comp universe spans 9 private firms (including Palantir, Waymo, Figure AI, Applied Intuition, and Skild AI) and 1 public comparable (Palantir, ticker PLTR) within the AI & Software sector.

Stage-progression analysis is the primary applicable method here. Using the $11M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A progression yields an implied valuation of $22M. A peer-median approach was not viable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within a narrow, defensible peer set; while 13 same-sector peers exist, fewer than three met tighter filtration criteria for stage and valuation tier.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $19M–$25M, with a median of $22M. This range is derived entirely from the stage-progression method given the limitations of peer comparables for this specific stage and company profile. Key uncertainties include the absence of robust same-stage peer data and the reliance on a single analytical method. Future rounds and milestone achievement will provide better calibration against emerging frontier-tech benchmarks.
